title: genista sagittalis subsp. undulata
intro: Genista sagittalis subsp. undulata, also known as the bent broom or undulate broom, is a subspecies of the broom plant Genista sagittalis, which is a shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Genista sagittalis subsp. undulata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antifungal agent. Genista sagittalis subsp. undulata is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
